cycle_label: GOV-04 — governance-thread standalone sprint · pool telemetry validation + first batch ownerless-work execution · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-04 = 4). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-04 CLOSED 2026-05-14, all four loops clean. L01 ran a fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep + established pool telemetry (cycle.json tracks pool-item state in real-time). L02 ran the first pool batch (WS-B2 deferred + XDG-portal struck — triage pattern validated). L03 surfaced the key finding: pushing the thread's commits revealed the cycle-29 nephew trio had already done backlog items #3/#4-F4/#5 on the dacumen-repo surface — the nephew-trio-vs-standalone-GOV structure question was a false binary, both ran and split the backlog by surface. L04 routed out the remaining instrument-gaps F2/F3 (telemetry-contract surface — nephew-owned, not GOV-shaped) and closed the sprint. No carryover, no escalations, standing watches green. GOV-05 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled.
goals: L01 — fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep. DONE. Headline: cycle-09 workspace has two ancient deferred ownerless items (WS-B2 Nextcloud content-sync, XDG portal systemd units). Also found: cycle-29 in-scope governance items (3 sync-mechanism holes, instrument-gaps F2/F3/F4, DellaTech externalizables) queued behind initial pool-batch work. Pool telemetry established via cycle.json carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 section.

L02 — first pool batch execution (WS-B2, XDG-portal). DONE. WS-B2 triaged + DEFERRED (valid substrate work, but 20+ cycle deferral + no critical blocker → context note written for cycle-30+ recovery). XDG-portal verified orphaned (zero code refs, zero service deps, failed 1.5w) → masked both units to /dev/null + documented as struck. Pool-telemetry validated: real-time tracking through cycle.json carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 confirmed ergonomic.

L03 — surface-division finding + standing watches. DONE. Pushing the standalone thread's commits to dacumen.git surfaced that the cycle-29 nephew trio already completed GOV-04 backlog items #3 (3 sync-mechanism holes, dacumen v0.2.8), #4-F4 (cross-sprint-audit bug, v0.2.8), and #5 (DellaTech externalizables, v0.2.9). This resolves cycle-29's open structure question: nephew-trio-vs-standalone-GOV was a false binary — both ran, splitting the backlog by SURFACE (trio → dacumen-repo docs/ADRs/scripts; GOV thread → homelab-infra + pool telemetry), zero file collisions. Standing watches both GREEN.

L04 — F2/F3 route-out + GOV-04 close. DONE. Remaining instrument-gaps F2 (telemetry-contract checker unscheduled) + F3 (2 failing contracts) ROUTED OUT — not GOV-shaped: they sit on the telemetry-contract surface the cycle-29 nephew trio already owns. Routed with a paper trail (same discipline as GOV-02/GOV-03 route-outs). GOV-04 closed, status set to closed. No carryover — GOV-05 scopes from a fresh sweep.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-04 is CLOSED — all four loops clean, no carryover, no escalations, nothing on fire. This cycle.json sits in the between-sprints state until GOV-05 opens. To open GOV-05: run a fresh health-check sweep FIRST (per the operating model — scope from the sweep, never from a hunch or a carryover list). The sweep covers: systemctl --user services + timers (look for failed/stale units), observatory data freshness, the casey-pipeline trigger path, cross-BU service health (dev + prod casey-junior, rag indexers), and the health-refresh-check.timer status (it's now a standing instrument — a red checker is a real finding). Then scope GOV-05's queue from what the sweep surfaces, re-author this plain_english block, and flip status → open. Known item for the sweep to re-test: instrument-gaps F2 (telemetry-contract checker unscheduled) + F3 (2 failing contracts) — routed out of GOV-04 to the telemetry-contract surface owner; if still ownerless, they re-surface as genuinely GOV-shaped.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-04 opened after GOV-03 closed fully (health-refresh failure signal installed, NCAA baseball archived). Scoped from a fresh cycle-29 governance-backlog sweep per the operating model. Govenance-thread now registered in casey-junior PROJECT_ENDPOINTS for ops-dashboard visibility (deployment_id: 09c22323).
stakes: The governance thread is cross-BU and standalone — nesting its sprint-logs inside one BU's foreman (darntech) stranded them when the active cycle moved, and made /brief unrunnable from the governance workspace. Its own foreman-enabled home makes the thread continuously briefable and parallel-runnable alongside whatever product cycle is active. GOV-04 establishes pool-telemetry patterns (machine-readable work-item tracking in cycle.json) to make governance work-item progression visible for sweeps + dashboards.
notes: GOV-04 closed 2026-05-14, all four loops clean — same-day open-to-close. L01 sweep + pool telemetry, L02 first pool batch (WS-B2 deferred, XDG-portal struck), L03 surface-division finding, L04 F2/F3 route-out + close. Key durable finding: nephew-trio-vs-standalone-GOV is a false binary — they self-partition by surface (dacumen-repo vs homelab-infra). Governance-thread registered as Casey Junior deployment 09c22323 for ops-dashboard visibility. The governance-thread workspace is a checkout of dacumen.git — governance artifacts (charter amendments, ADRs) and governance operational tracking (.foreman + GOV sprint-logs) both belong to the dacumen repo. GOV-05 opens from a fresh health-check sweep when next scheduled; carries nothing forward (GOV-02 lesson: carryover backlogs rot).

carryover_decisions_at_open.gov03_dellatech_rag_indexer_watch: GOV-03 L01 found dellatech-rag-indexer.service never run (LAST = -). L04 verified: first fire (2026-05-15 02:32) succeeded cleanly, 1.9s, indexed 33 chunks. RESOLVED GREEN — no escalation needed.
carryover_decisions_at_open.gov03_health_refresh_wrapper_first_cron_: GOV-03 L03 rewrote 6 health-refresh crontab lines to use health-refresh-run.sh. L04 manually verified: all 6 pipelines reported 'last success 0h ago' (from L03 priming, still fresh). Checker exited 0 (all 26h-fresh). First real automatic exercise (tomorrow's 07:15-07:35 cron batch) will write new heartbeat timestamps. WATCHING but no escalation expected.
carryover_decisions_at_open.cycle_29_carryover_pool_telemetry: GOV-04 L01 established pool-telemetry tracking in darntech cycle-29 cycle.json: carryover_resolved_in_cycle_29 section now machine-reads work-item state. WS-B2 (Nextcloud content-sync) + XDG-portal units selected as L02 test batch. After L02 completion, operator decides pace for pooling remaining cycle-29 governance items (sync-holes, instrument-gaps, externalizables).
carryover_decisions_at_open.cascade_rc_rename_consumer_runtime_gap: Memory codified during GOV-02 L04: RC6 package rename not 'landed' until consumer venvs reconciled, not just source + image. Applies whenever a package rename touches deployed services + dev environments.
